patch.value: Drastically improve GitHub runner time and resource usage (free-tier only)

Current CI per-commit cost: 5 matrix jobs running ~11-12min each (~700s wall). Goal: cut total runner minutes 40-60% without losing test coverage, platform matrix, or quality gates. Constraint: free-tier GitHub features only - no paid runners, no GitHub Hosted Larger Runners. Approach: skip-on-doc-only, eliminate duplicate test runs, smaller PR matrix, tsbuildinfo incremental cache, parallelize gates into their own short job, cache vitest/lancedb/sentry caches between runs.

Average CI duration per push/PR cut by >=40%; total runner minutes per PR cut by >=50% on common docs-only or single-file PRs; full matrix still runs on main push; coverage gate, static quality gate, plugin smoke tests still gating; pnpm audit/security:scan still gating; no paid GitHub features used; CI is green on the change.

author: codex-agent
message: Claim CI performance parent for latest free-tier optimization pass
